Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: the user has provided a detailed description of a multinational supply chain for smartphone manufacturing and asked for 20 thought provoking open ended questions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The questions should aim to uncover insights potential issues improvement strategies and decision implications

Name: Validation - PE
Text: First I need to ensure the questions are truly open ended and thought provoking not just superficial inquiries

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: They should encourage deep analysis of the process

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The user emphasized no SQL queries so Ill focus solely on qualitative questions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The supply chain involves multiple components R and D sourcing manufacturing logistics distribution and post sales support

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I should cover each area while also considering cross cutting themes like risk management sustainability and technology integration

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For sourcing questions about supplier diversification and geopolitical risks come to mind

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Manufacturing raises points about automation and labor practices

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Logistics involves trade offs between cost and speed

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Distribution needs questions about channel strategies and inventory optimization

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I also want to include questions about broader implications like how data analytics can transform decision making or how regulations across regions create complexity

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: The questions should challenge assumptions and explore what if scenarios to help the user think beyond the obvious

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Ill structure them to flow logically from upstream to downstream but the order isnt critical as long as theyre diverse and insightful

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me draft them now making sure each one prompts reflection rather than a simple yes no answer
